steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence opens with: "A recipient of the Nobel Prize in Chemistry and the Albert Einstein World Award of Science, ______ known as femtochemistry..." The opening phrase is a dangling modifier — a noun phrase that renames the subject. The rule is: the noun or noun phrase immediately after the comma must be the person or thing the opening phrase describes. Because the opening phrase describes a person (the recipient of those awards), the subject after the comma must be that person.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• "the limits of measurability were expanded by chemist Ahmed Z who introduced a new scientific discipline": the subject after the comma is "the limits of measurability," not a person. The modifier dangles. ✗
  • "chemist Ahmed Zewail expanded the limits of measurability and introduced a new scientific discipline": the subject after the comma is "chemist Ahmed Zewail," the person described by the opening phrase. Modifier is correctly attached. ✓
  • "the introduction of a new scientific discipline by chemist Ahmed Zewail led to the expansion of the limits of measurability": the subject after the comma is "the introduction," not a person. The modifier dangles. ✗
  • "the result of chemist Ahmed Zewail's expansion of the limits of measurability was the introduction of a new scientific discipline": the subject after the comma is "the result," not a person. The modifier dangles. ✗
That matches the correct answer — B is correct.
